steps.title: Welcome to Day 27 — Building Your AI-Powered Career
steps.body: AI is changing every industry. Some people worry about this. You are not one of them — because you have spent three weeks learning to use AI effectively. Today you learn how to use AI to accelerate your career: auditing your skills, building your brand, networking smarter, and preparing for a job market that increasingly values AI literacy.

steps.title: AI in the workplace — the new reality
steps.body: The jobs least affected by AI are those requiring high human judgment, empathy, physical presence, and creativity. The jobs most affected are those with repetitive mental tasks. Here is what this means for you: people who use AI effectively will outpace those who do not. Not because AI replaces them — but because AI makes them faster, smarter, and more capable. You are already ahead by being here.

steps.title: Run your career audit

fields.label: What is your current role or desired role?
fields.placeholder: marketing coordinator aspiring to be a marketing manager

fields.label: What are your top strengths and skills?
fields.placeholder: writing, project management, social media, data analysis basics

fields.label: What skills do you feel you are missing?
fields.placeholder: advanced analytics, team leadership, public speaking

steps.template: I am a {{role}}. My strengths: {{strengths}}. My skill gaps: {{gaps}}. (1) For each of my skill gaps, suggest specific AI-supercharged ways to learn or fill them in under 30 days. (2) Which of my strengths could AI amplify the most? (3) What new roles or opportunities open up if I combine my strengths with AI skills? (4) Give me a 90-day career development plan using AI as my accelerator.

steps.title: Save your career audit
steps.body: Paste your career audit. This is your roadmap for the next 90 days.

steps.title: The career pivot — Anita's move
steps.body: Anita has been in customer service for eight years. She wants to move into a product management role but has no direct experience. AI helps her: (1) Map her existing skills (communication, problem-solving, data analysis) to product management requirements. (2) Identify the one skill gap to close first. (3) Draft a compelling story connecting her background to the new role. (4) Generate interview answers that reframe her experience. She lands a product role in 3 months.

steps.title: Skill gap analysis — knowing what to learn
steps.body: Instead of guessing what skills you need, ask AI. Prompt: 'I want to move from [current role] to [target role]. List the top 5 skills I need that I likely do not have. For each skill: (1) Why it matters. (2) How to learn it with AI in under 30 days. (3) How to prove it to employers (a project, certification, or portfolio piece).' This removes the guesswork from career growth.

steps.title: Career strategy check
steps.question: What is the best way to use AI when pivoting to a new career?
options: Ask AI to apply to jobs for you
Use AI to map your existing skills to the new role, identify gaps, and reframe your experience
Ignore your past experience and start from scratch
feedback: AI cannot apply for jobs yet — and you should still own your applications.
Correct. AI helps you connect what you already know to where you want to go.
Your past experience is valuable — you need to reframe it, not discard it.

steps.title: Personal branding with AI
steps.body: Your personal brand is what people say about you when you are not in the room. AI can help you shape it. Prompt: 'I want to build a personal brand as a [role/field]. My strengths are [list]. My audience is [target audience]. Help me: (1) Define my brand in one sentence. (2) Write a compelling LinkedIn headline and about section. (3) Suggest 5 content ideas that showcase my expertise. (4) Create a 30-day content plan.' Whether you want a new job, clients, or recognition — a strong brand opens doors.

steps.title: Build your AI-powered resume and portfolio

fields.label: Describe your current or most recent role
fields.placeholder: managed social media accounts for a mid-size company

fields.label: What is a key achievement you are proud of?
What role are you targeting?
fields.placeholder: social media manager or digital marketing specialist

steps.template: Help me build my career materials. Current role: {{current_role_desc}}. Key achievement: {{achievement}}. Target role: {{target_role}}. (1) Rewrite my achievement using strong action verbs and measurable results. (2) Write a professional summary for my resume that connects my experience to my target role. (3) Suggest a portfolio project I could build using AI that would impress hiring managers. (4) Give me three talking points for interviews.

steps.title: Save your career materials
steps.body: Paste your resume summary, portfolio ideas, and interview talking points.

steps.title: Networking with AI — smarter connections
steps.body: Networking feels awkward for most people. AI removes the awkwardness. Prompt: 'I want to connect with [type of people — product managers, designers, founders]. Draft a personalized LinkedIn connection request that is respectful, specific, and offers value. Also suggest 3 thoughtful questions I could ask in a conversation with them.' Or: 'I am attending [conference/event]. Help me identify 5 people I should talk to and prepare opening lines for each.'

questions.label: Where do you see your career in one year? How does AI fit into that vision?
How would you rate your AI literacy compared to peers in your field?
options: Way ahead — I can use AI for multiple tasks effectively
Comfortable — I know the basics and can learn more
Getting there — I understand the potential
Just starting — but I am here to learn

steps.title: Interview prep — ace your next interview
steps.body: Interviews are nerve-wracking, but AI makes preparation thorough and fast. Prompt: 'I am interviewing for [role] at [company]. Here is my resume: [paste]. Give me: (1) 10 likely questions based on my background. (2) Strong answers using the STAR method. (3) 3 questions I should ask the interviewer. (4) A one-minute pitch about who I am and what I bring.' Practice out loud. Then ask AI for feedback on your recorded practice answers.

questions.question: What is the first step when pivoting to a new career with AI?
options: Map your existing skills to the new role and identify skill gaps

questions.question: How can AI help with networking?
options: AI connects to people automatically on your behalf
AI drafts personalized messages and suggests conversation starters
AI replaces the need for human networking

questions.question: People who use AI effectively in the workplace will likely outpace those who do not.

steps.title: Day 27 complete — Career Builder
steps.body: You can now audit your career, identify skill gaps, build your personal brand, network smarter, and prepare for interviews — all with AI. Tomorrow is graduation — your final day of the entire program!
